title: Aggregate cancer-incidence teaching dataset (derived, k>=5)
context: Epidemiology and incidence analysis are important cancer data skills, but published aggregate incidence data (SEER/GLOBOCAN-style tables) are already public and open-access, making them viable for a derived teaching dataset. This is the first 'derived-from-open' epidemiology task: it takes published aggregate incidence tables (not individual records), applies the k>=5 cell-size check, and produces a teaching-scale table paired with exercises on interpreting incidence rates, age-standardization, and cross-tabulations. Risk tier is medium because this involves license verification (attribution propagation) and de-identification judgment (k>=5 check) on derived data from real public sources. Depends on catalog-010 (approved source) and snapshot-011 (license snapshot).
objective: Produce an aggregate cancer-incidence teaching dataset derived from published open-access aggregate incidence tables (SEER/GLOBOCAN-style), with k>=5 respected on all cells, full gate artifact, attribution, and Zenodo DOI.
acceptanceCriteria: Source is published aggregate incidence (SEER/GLOBOCAN-style), open-access, no controlled-access lineage; gate-002 PASS with license id+URL+snapshot committed.
Published at the source's aggregation level or coarser; every cross-tab cell >= 5 (k>=5 recorded in the gate artifact); no re-identification path; domain reviewer confirms de-id adequacy.
outputLicense propagated/compatible with source (CC-BY-4.0 or public-domain as applicable); attribution string recorded; riskTier:medium.
Data dictionary + Datasheet-for-Datasets + Croissant metadata valid + >= 1 exercise; completeness >= 90/100; Zenodo DOI minted.
Safety reviewer and domain reviewer sign-off on gate artifact and de-id adequacy.
resources: PLAN.md (§Data, licensing & compliance — Derived mode; §Solution — Dataset-inspection access protocol)
output: An aggregate cancer-incidence teaching dataset (CSV/TSV) derived from open-access aggregate tables, with gate PASS artifact, k>=5 compliance record, attribution, Croissant metadata, data dictionary, Zenodo DOI, and >= 1 teaching exercise.
